GuiHelpGettingStartedExpertHelp: Use Help for reference topics, logs, and recovery guidance.
GuiHelpGettingStartedExpertMain: Use Start Guide on the main screen if you want the guided setup workflow.
GuiHelpGettingStartedExpertTabs: Otherwise jump directly to the tab you need and review the selection there.
GuiHelpGettingStartedSafeHelp: Use Help when you want reference material instead of the guided setup flow.
GuiHelpGettingStartedSafeMain: Use Start Guide on the main screen when you want the first-run walkthrough.
GuiHelpGettingStartedSafePreview: Use Preview Run before applying anything so you can review the impact first.
GuiHelpGettingStartedStdHelp: Use Help later when you need to rediscover the available workflows.
GuiHelpGettingStartedStdMain: Use Start Guide on the main screen for the guided onboarding flow.
GuiHelpGettingStartedStdPreview: Preview Run lets you review what will change before you apply it.
GuiHelpRemoteCliPreview: Remote management currently uses PowerShell Remoting through the CLI only. The GUI does not yet provide a remote connection or session workflow.
GuiHelpRemoteExpertRequirements: Enterprise readiness still requires an explicit connection layer, reusable session tracking, visible target context, per-machine execution reporting, and aggregated remote logs.
GuiHelpRemoteManagedDevices: Managed, work, school, or domain-enrolled devices should be reviewed with the appropriate admin team before remote runs.
